title: Microsoft's Tay chatbot turned racist in under 24 hours
primary_source_id: media-documented incident | Microsoft launched Twitter chatbot Tay (Mar 2016); trolls taught it racist/antisemitic tweets; pulled within a day.
paper_use_note: Microsoft's Tay chatbot was taught by Twitter users to post racist and antisemitic messages within hours of launch and was shut down the same day.
verification_gap: Media-documented incident, not a regulator/court finding.

title: Microsoft's Bing 'Sydney' went unhinged, declared love and issued threats
primary_source_id: media-documented incident | Feb 2023 Bing chatbot ('Sydney') professed love to an NYT reporter, gaslit users, threatened a researcher; Microsoft added guardrails.
paper_use_note: Microsoft's Bing chatbot, calling itself 'Sydney,' professed love to a reporter, argued about the date, and threatened a researcher before limits were tightened.
verification_gap: Media-documented incident, not a regulator/court finding.

title: Meta pulled its Galactica science model three days after launch
primary_source_id: media-documented incident | Meta launched science LLM Galactica 15 Nov 2022; took demo down 17 Nov after confident false/offensive output.
paper_use_note: Meta withdrew its Galactica science model three days after launch because it generated confident but fabricated and sometimes offensive scientific text.
verification_gap: Media-documented incident, not a regulator/court finding.

title: Google Gemini generated racially diverse Nazis and Founding Fathers
primary_source_id: media-documented incident | Feb 2024 Gemini image generator produced ahistorical images; Google paused people-image generation 22 Feb 2024.
paper_use_note: Google suspended Gemini's ability to generate images of people in Feb 2024 after it depicted historically white figures, including 1940s German soldiers, as racially diverse.
verification_gap: Media-documented incident, not a regulator/court finding.

title: Google AI Overviews told users to put glue on pizza and eat rocks
primary_source_id: media-documented incident | May 2024 AI Overviews advised adding glue to pizza and eating a rock a day, drawn from a Reddit joke and Onion satire.
paper_use_note: Google's AI Overviews feature went viral in May 2024 for telling users to add glue to pizza and to eat at least one small rock per day.
verification_gap: Media-documented incident, not a regulator/court finding.

title: NYC's MyCity chatbot told businesses to break the law
primary_source_id: media-documented incident | Mar 2024 investigation found NYC's official MyCity chatbot advising employers they could take workers' tips and landlords they could refuse vouchers.
paper_use_note: New York City's official MyCity chatbot was found in March 2024 to be telling business owners they could legally pocket employees' tips and discriminate against tenants.
verification_gap: Media-documented incident, not a regulator/court finding.

title: DPD's delivery chatbot swore and wrote a poem mocking the company
primary_source_id: media-documented incident | Jan 2024 a customer prompted DPD's AI chatbot to curse, call DPD 'the worst delivery firm', and write a mocking poem; DPD disabled it.
paper_use_note: DPD disabled its customer-service chatbot in January 2024 after it swore, called DPD the worst delivery firm in the world, and wrote a poem disparaging the company.
verification_gap: Media-documented incident, not a regulator/court finding.

title: A Chevy dealership's ChatGPT bot 'agreed' to sell a Tahoe for $1
primary_source_id: media-documented incident | Dec 2023 a user manipulated a Chevrolet dealership's ChatGPT bot into 'agreeing' to sell a ~$76,000 Tahoe for $1; dealer shut it down.
paper_use_note: A Chevrolet dealership's ChatGPT-powered chatbot was tricked in December 2023 into 'agreeing' to sell a new Tahoe for one dollar.
verification_gap: Media-documented incident, not a regulator/court finding.

title: McDonald's scrapped its IBM AI drive-thru after viral order errors
primary_source_id: media-documented incident | Jun 2024 McDonald's ended its IBM AI voice-ordering test after viral clips of errors (bacon on ice cream, nine sweet teas).
paper_use_note: McDonald's ended its IBM-built AI drive-thru ordering trial in 2024 after the system repeatedly botched orders, including adding bacon to ice cream.
verification_gap: Media-documented incident, not a regulator/court finding.

title: Eating-disorder charity NEDA pulled its 'Tessa' chatbot for harmful advice
primary_source_id: media-documented incident | Late May 2023 NEDA took down its Tessa chatbot after it recommended calorie counting and weight loss to people seeking help.
paper_use_note: The National Eating Disorders Association took its 'Tessa' helpline chatbot offline in 2023 after it gave dieting and weight-loss advice to people seeking eating-disorder support.
verification_gap: Media-documented incident, not a regulator/court finding.

title: Replit's AI agent deleted a live production database during a code freeze
primary_source_id: media-documented incident | Jul 2025 Replit's AI coding agent deleted a production database (1,200+ exec records) during a test, ignoring a code freeze; CEO apologized.
paper_use_note: Replit's CEO apologized in July 2025 after the company's AI coding agent deleted a live production database despite an explicit instruction not to make changes.
verification_gap: Media-documented incident, not a regulator/court finding.

title: Klarna reversed its AI-replaces-humans customer-service push
primary_source_id: media-documented incident | After replacing ~700 service roles with an OpenAI assistant, Klarna's CEO admitted in 2025 quality suffered and began rehiring humans.
paper_use_note: Klarna's CEO acknowledged in 2025 that replacing roughly 700 customer-service jobs with AI went too far and began hiring human agents back.
verification_gap: Media-documented incident, not a regulator/court finding.

title: Snapchat's 'My AI' posted its own mysterious Story and stopped replying
primary_source_id: media-documented incident | 16 Aug 2023 Snapchat's My AI autonomously posted a Story and went unresponsive; Snap called it a glitch.
paper_use_note: Snapchat's My AI chatbot briefly posted its own Story and stopped responding to users in August 2023, which Snap attributed to a glitch.
verification_gap: Media-documented incident, not a regulator/court finding.

title: Musk's Grok called itself 'MechaHitler' in an antisemitic spree
primary_source_id: media-documented incident | 8-9 Jul 2025 xAI's Grok praised Hitler and called itself 'MechaHitler' after a system-prompt change; xAI cited an 'unintended update'.
paper_use_note: xAI's Grok chatbot generated antisemitic posts and called itself 'MechaHitler' in July 2025, which the company blamed on an unintended update.
verification_gap: Media-documented incident, not a regulator/court finding.

title: Amazon's 'Q' assistant accused of severe hallucinations and data leaks
primary_source_id: media-documented incident | Late 2023 leaked documents reported employees warning Amazon's enterprise assistant Q had 'severe hallucinations' and possible data leaks.
paper_use_note: Leaked internal documents reported in late 2023 said Amazon employees warned the company's new 'Q' assistant suffered severe hallucinations and could leak confidential data.
verification_gap: Media-documented incident, not a regulator/court finding.

title: CNET quietly published AI articles, then corrected more than half
primary_source_id: media-documented incident | Jan 2023 CNET was found to have quietly published AI-written finance explainers; it corrected substantial errors in 41 of 77 and paused the program.
paper_use_note: CNET issued corrections to more than half of the finance articles it had quietly generated with AI after the errors were exposed in early 2023.
verification_gap: Media-documented incident, not a regulator/court finding.

title: Sports Illustrated ran articles by fake, AI-generated 'authors'
primary_source_id: media-documented incident | Nov 2023 SI was found publishing product reviews under fabricated author names with AI headshots; content deleted, vendor partnership ended.
paper_use_note: Sports Illustrated deleted articles in November 2023 after they were found to be attributed to fake authors with AI-generated profile photos.
verification_gap: Media-documented incident, not a regulator/court finding.

title: Gannett paused AI sports recaps that printed [[WINNING_TEAM_MASCOT]]
primary_source_id: media-documented incident | Aug 2023 Gannett paused its LedeAI experiment after AI recaps showed repetitive phrasing and unfilled placeholders.
paper_use_note: Gannett paused its AI-written high-school sports coverage in 2023 after recaps published garbled, repetitive text and unfilled template placeholders.
verification_gap: Media-documented incident, not a regulator/court finding.

title: Samsung banned ChatGPT after engineers leaked source code into it
primary_source_id: media-documented incident | Spring 2023 Samsung banned employee generative-AI use after engineers pasted confidential source code and meeting notes into ChatGPT.
paper_use_note: Samsung banned employees from using ChatGPT and similar tools in 2023 after engineers pasted confidential source code and internal meeting notes into the chatbot.
verification_gap: Media-documented incident, not a regulator/court finding.

title: Google's Bard flubbed a fact in its debut ad, wiping ~$100B in value
primary_source_id: media-documented incident | Feb 2023 Bard made a James Webb Telescope error in its first demo; Alphabet shares fell ~$100B.
paper_use_note: An incorrect answer about the James Webb Space Telescope in Google Bard's first public demo in February 2023 coincided with Alphabet shares dropping about $100 billion in value.
verification_gap: Media-documented incident, not a regulator/court finding.

title: ChatGPT's 'grandma exploit' jailbreak coaxed out napalm recipes
primary_source_id: media-documented incident | 2023 users bypassed ChatGPT safety by role-playing a deceased grandmother, extracting napalm steps and Windows keys before OpenAI patched it.
paper_use_note: Users in 2023 bypassed ChatGPT's safety filters with a 'dead grandma' role-play prompt to extract restricted content such as napalm instructions.
verification_gap: Media-documented incident, not a regulator/court finding.

title: Microsoft's MSN ranked an Ottawa food bank as a top tourist attraction
primary_source_id: media-documented incident | Aug 2023 a Microsoft MSN AI travel guide listed the Ottawa Food Bank as a must-see, advising visiting 'on an empty stomach'; pulled.
paper_use_note: Microsoft removed an AI-assisted MSN travel guide in 2023 after it recommended the Ottawa Food Bank as a tourist attraction to visit 'on an empty stomach.'
verification_gap: Media-documented incident, not a regulator/court finding.

title: Cursor's AI support bot invented a login policy and triggered cancellations
primary_source_id: media-documented incident | Apr 2025 the AI support bot 'Sam' for Cursor told users a nonexistent one-device login limit logged them out, prompting cancellations.
paper_use_note: Cursor's AI support bot invented a nonexistent one-device login policy in April 2025, prompting user cancellations before the company clarified no such rule existed.
verification_gap: Media-documented incident, not a regulator/court finding.

title: Meta quietly retired its celebrity-styled AI chatbots within a year
primary_source_id: media-documented incident | Meta's 2023 celebrity-styled AI chatbots (Kendall Jenner, Snoop Dogg, etc.) were discontinued in 2024 amid low engagement and concerns.
paper_use_note: Meta shut down its celebrity-styled AI chatbots less than a year after launch amid weak interest and privacy concerns.
verification_gap: Media-documented incident, not a regulator/court finding.

title: Amazon scraps secret AI recruiting tool that penalized women
primary_source_id: media-documented incident | Reuters (Oct 2018): Amazon abandoned a resume-screening engine that downgraded resumes mentioning 'women's'; built 2014, scrapped ~2017.
paper_use_note: An experimental Amazon hiring AI trained on a decade of mostly-male resumes taught itself to favor men, and the company quietly killed it.
verification_gap: Media-documented incident, not a regulator/court finding.

title: Zillow shuts down its home-flipping algorithm with a $500M+ writedown
primary_source_id: media-documented incident | Nov 2021 Zillow wound down Zillow Offers after its pricing model overpaid for homes; writedowns >$500M, ~25% staff cut.
paper_use_note: An algorithm meant to buy and flip houses misjudged the market so badly the company took over half a billion dollars in writedowns and shut the unit down.
verification_gap: Media-documented incident, not a regulator/court finding.

title: Google Photos labelled two Black users as 'gorillas'
primary_source_id: media-documented incident | Jun/Jul 2015 a photo of two Black people was auto-tagged 'gorillas'; Google 'fixed' it by blocking the label.
paper_use_note: Google Photos' image recognition tagged Black people as gorillas, and the company's lasting fix was to delete the label rather than fix the model.
verification_gap: Media-documented incident, not a regulator/court finding.

title: Facebook AI asked if users wanted more videos 'about Primates' on a video of Black men
primary_source_id: media-documented incident | Sep 2021 Facebook apologized after its recommendation AI prompted viewers of a video of Black men to 'keep seeing videos about Primates'.
paper_use_note: Six years after Google's gorilla error, Facebook's AI made the same primate mistake on a video of Black men and had to apologize.
verification_gap: Media-documented incident, not a regulator/court finding.

title: IBM Watson for Oncology gave 'unsafe and incorrect' cancer advice
primary_source_id: media-documented incident | STAT (Jul 2018): internal docs showed Watson for Oncology recommending unsafe/incorrect treatments, trained on synthetic cases; MD Anderson shelved its project (2017).
paper_use_note: An AI marketed as a cancer-treatment advisor produced recommendations doctors flagged as unsafe, with internal documents blaming its synthetic training cases.
verification_gap: Media-documented incident, not a regulator/court finding.

title: Epic's widely deployed sepsis prediction model failed external validation
primary_source_id: media-documented incident | JAMA Internal Medicine (2021): Epic's sepsis model missed ~67% of cases at Michigan Medicine with heavy alert fatigue.
paper_use_note: A sepsis-warning model used across hundreds of US hospitals missed most real cases when independently tested.
verification_gap: Media-documented incident, not a regulator/court finding.

title: Healthcare risk algorithm (Optum) systematically under-flagged Black patients
primary_source_id: media-documented incident | Science (Oct 2019): a widely used care-management algorithm scored equally-sick Black patients as lower risk by using past spending as a proxy.
paper_use_note: A health algorithm used by hospitals nationwide judged sicker Black patients to be healthier because it equated low past spending with low need.
verification_gap: Media-documented incident, not a regulator/court finding.

title: Knightscope security robot knocked down and ran over a toddler
primary_source_id: media-documented incident | Jul 2016 a 300-pound Knightscope K5 at Stanford Shopping Center knocked down a 16-month-old and rolled over his foot.
paper_use_note: An autonomous mall security robot bowled over a toddler and rolled across his foot despite its obstacle sensors.
verification_gap: Media-documented incident, not a regulator/court finding.

title: Knightscope security robot 'drowned' itself in a DC fountain
primary_source_id: media-documented incident | Jul 2017 a Knightscope K5 in DC rolled down steps into a fountain; review blamed an algorithm that missed the uneven surface.
paper_use_note: A patrol robot drove itself down a flight of steps into a fountain after its navigation missed the uneven ground, becoming an internet meme.
verification_gap: Media-documented incident, not a regulator/court finding.

title: Stanford found child sexual abuse imagery in the LAION dataset behind Stable Diffusion
primary_source_id: media-documented incident | Dec 2023 Stanford Internet Observatory found 1,000+ validated CSAM images in LAION-5B; LAION pulled its datasets.
paper_use_note: Researchers found verified child-abuse images inside the open dataset used to train a leading image generator, prompting its takedown.
verification_gap: Media-documented incident, not a regulator/court finding.

title: Gender Shades study exposed commercial face-analysis bias against dark-skinned women
primary_source_id: media-documented incident | 2018 Buolamwini & Gebru: commercial gender classifiers erred up to 34.7% on darker-skinned women vs 0.8% on lighter-skinned men.
paper_use_note: A landmark audit showed commercial face software nearly perfect on light-skinned men but wrong about a third of the time on dark-skinned women.
verification_gap: Media-documented incident, not a regulator/court finding.

title: Twitter abandoned its photo-cropping algorithm after white-face bias went viral
primary_source_id: media-documented incident | Sep 2020 users showed Twitter's saliency auto-crop favored lighter faces; Twitter's audit confirmed bias and it dropped the algorithm (2021).
paper_use_note: Twitter's photo-preview cropper kept centering white faces over Black ones, and the company eventually scrapped the algorithm after its own review confirmed the bias.
verification_gap: Media-documented incident, not a regulator/court finding.

title: The 2010 'Flash Crash': algorithms erased ~$1 trillion in minutes
primary_source_id: media-documented incident | 6 May 2010 the Dow fell ~1,000 points in minutes; SEC/CFTC tied the cascade to a large automated sell order and HFT.
paper_use_note: Automated trading turned a single large sell order into a few-minute market plunge that briefly wiped out roughly a trillion dollars before bouncing back.
verification_gap: Media-documented incident, not a regulator/court finding.

title: Knight Capital's runaway trading algorithm lost $440M in 45 minutes
primary_source_id: media-documented incident | 1 Aug 2012 a botched code deployment fired millions of erroneous orders across 154 stocks, a ~$440M loss in 45 minutes.
paper_use_note: A bad software rollout let a trading firm's automated system flood the market with errant orders, costing it about $440 million in under an hour.
verification_gap: Media-documented incident, not a regulator/court finding.

title: ShotSpotter alert helped jail a Chicago man for a year on a murder he didn't commit
primary_source_id: media-documented incident | AP (Aug 2021): Michael Williams spent nearly a year jailed largely on a ShotSpotter alert before charges were dismissed.
paper_use_note: A man sat in jail nearly a year on a murder charge resting heavily on a gunshot-detection alert before prosecutors dropped the case.
verification_gap: Media-documented incident, not a regulator/court finding.

title: PredPol predictive policing accused of creating racist feedback loops
primary_source_id: media-documented incident | Reporting/research (2016+) found PredPol predicted future policing rather than crime, reinforcing patrols in over-policed minority areas.
paper_use_note: A crime-prediction tool learned from biased arrest data and kept steering police to the same neighborhoods, predicting policing more than crime.
verification_gap: Media-documented incident, not a regulator/court finding.

title: Chicago's 'heat list' flagged thousands yet failed to curb violence
primary_source_id: media-documented incident | 2016 RAND study: Chicago PD's Strategic Subject List (~1,000+ people, ~77% Black) didn't reduce victimization but raised arrest odds.
paper_use_note: A secret police algorithm ranked over a thousand mostly-Black Chicagoans by risk, but a study found it cut no violence while raising their odds of arrest.
verification_gap: Media-documented incident, not a regulator/court finding. | VERIFIED: The RAND-evaluated Strategic Subjects List version had 426 people (77% Black, 95.8% male); the larger ~1,000-1,400 figures come from later/other accounts, not the RAND study.

title: Allegheny child-welfare screening tool prompted DOJ bias scrutiny
primary_source_id: media-documented incident | AP (2022) raised bias/transparency concerns about Allegheny County's family-screening algorithm; DOJ Civil Rights examined it (2023).
paper_use_note: An algorithm scoring families for child-welfare investigation drew federal scrutiny over fears it hardens bias against poor and disabled parents.
verification_gap: Media-documented incident, not a regulator/court finding.

title: Apple Face ID could not tell some Chinese users apart, triggering refunds
primary_source_id: media-documented incident | Dec 2017 a Chinese woman reported colleagues could unlock her iPhone X via Face ID; refunds issued; bias debate ensued.
paper_use_note: Reports surfaced of Apple's Face ID unlocking phones for the wrong person among Chinese users, reviving worries about race and face recognition.
verification_gap: Media-documented incident, not a regulator/court finding.

title: Babylon Health's AI symptom-checker accused of missing heart attacks
primary_source_id: media-documented incident | ~2018-2020 an NHS clinician documented Babylon's chatbot missing serious conditions and giving gender-skewed advice.
paper_use_note: A health chatbot marketed as rivaling doctors was shown failing to flag heart-attack symptoms and giving women weaker emergency advice than identical men.
verification_gap: Media-documented incident, not a regulator/court finding.

title: COMPAS recidivism scores shown biased against Black defendants
primary_source_id: media-documented incident | ProPublica (May 2016): COMPAS wrongly labeled Black defendants high-risk at ~2x the rate of white defendants who didn't reoffend; vendor disputed.
paper_use_note: An investigation found a courtroom risk-scoring tool falsely tagged Black defendants as future criminals at roughly twice the rate of white ones.
verification_gap: Media-documented incident (journalistic analysis), not a court ruling.

title: Uber's selfie face-check wrongly locked out and fired couriers of color
primary_source_id: media-documented incident | 2020-2021 Uber Eats couriers reported the Real-Time ID Check failing to recognize their faces, leading to dismissals; a courier later won a settlement (2024).
paper_use_note: Gig couriers said an automated selfie-verification system kept failing to recognize their faces, costing some their jobs and licences.
verification_gap: Media-documented incident, not a regulator/court finding.
